stem.rawText:
ВҠВҮВ–ВҷВ‘ВҲВ—Вҗ В–ВӢВ‘ВҗВ•вҖ«ЬЁвҖ¬бҲәЭҠбҲ»ВғВҗВҶвҖ«Ь©вҖ¬бҲәЭҠбҲ»ВғВ”ВҮВҶВҮВҲВӢВҗВҮВҶВғВ•ВҲВ‘ВҺВҺВ‘ВҷВ•ВҲВ‘В”В’В‘В•ВӢВ–ВӢВҳВҮВӢВҗВ–ВҮВүВҮВ”В•ЭҠЗЈ

                                                      аҜҘаӯҖа¬ө

       ВҠВғВ–ВӢВ•В–ВҠВҮВ•ВҸВғВҺВҺВҮВ•В–В’В‘В•ВӢВ–ВӢВҳВҮВӢВҗВ–ВҮВүВҮВ”ЭҠВ•В— ВҠВ–ВҠВғВ–вҖ«Ь©вҖ¬бҲәЭҠбҲ» аөҗ 150З«

solution.rawText:
It seems that we need to calculate F(n) before we can find G(n). We could do this either using
integration or geometry. We start with an integration method:
                                            1 n
                                              Z
                                    F(n) =       (n вҲ’ x) dx
                                           n 0
                                                        n
                                         = n1 nx вҲ’ 12 x2 0
                                             

                                                = n1 (n2 вҲ’ 21 n2 )
                                                = 12 n

We could reach the same conclusion by sketching a graph of the function:

                                            y

                                        n




                                                                      x
                                            0              n


The area of this triangle is 12 n2 , so F(n) = n1 Г— 12 n2 = 12 n
We now have:
                                                         n
                                                         X
                                            G(n) =             F(r)
                                                         r=1
                                                         Xn
                                                               1
                                                     =         2r
                                                       r=1
                                                          Xn
                                                     = 12     r
                                                          r=1

where for the last line we have used ( 21 F(1) + 21 F(2) + В· В· В· ) = 12 (F(1) + F(2) + В· В· В· )
Now nr=1 r = 1 + 2 + В· В· В· + n = 21 n(n + 1), either using the formula for triangular numbers or
     P
of the sum of an arithmetic series.
So G(n) = 41 n(n + 1).
Therefore

                                                         G(n) > 150
                                                   1
                                        вҮҗвҮ’         4 n(n + 1) > 150
                                        вҮҗвҮ’          n(n + 1) > 600

So we can try values: 25 is a nice number to calculate with, so we start with options C and D:

                                             24 Г— 25 = 600
                                             25 Г— 26 = 650

so n = 25 is the smallest positive integer for which G(n) > 150, and the answer is D.
